Summary: In mice Runx2 is the first transcription factor directing multipotent mesenchymal cells to the osteoblast lineage. During endochondral ossification Ihh from prehypertrophic chondrocytes is required for Runx2 expression in perichondrial mesenchyme. Runx2 induces Sp7 to generate Runx2+ Sp7+ preosteoblasts, and canonical Wnt is required for these preosteoblasts to become immature osteoblasts expressing Col1a1 and Spp1. Runx2 and Sp7 are required for immature osteoblasts to mature, marked by Bglap/Bglap2 and high Col1a1. Runx2 is expressed across the osteoblast lineage from osteoprogenitors to osteocytes. In calvarial and suture mesenchyme Runx2 induces Hedgehog, Fgf, Wnt, Pthlh genes and Dlx5 to drive osteoblast differentiation. More than half normal Runx2 dosage was required for induction in suture mesenchymal cells while half dosage sufficed in committed osteoblasts. Fgf stabilizes Runx2 via Mapk1/3 phosphorylation. Germline deletion of Runx2 results in complete absence of intramembranous and endochondral bone.
References: Komori, T. (2024). Regulation of Skeletal Development and Maintenance by Runx2 and Sp7. Int J Mol Sci, 25(18), 10102. https://doi.org/10.3390/ijms251810102
